name: Dead Hang
resistance_profile: Static hold, so grip tension is even for the whole hang — bodyweight loads the finger flexors at one fixed length rather than through a range.
cues: Hang from a bar, full grip
execution_steps: Set a pull-up bar within reach when your arms are fully extended overhead (stand on a box if needed).
Grip the bar with both hands, palms facing away, about shoulder-width apart.
Step or lift your feet off so you hang with straight arms, keeping your shoulders slightly engaged rather than fully slumped.
Hold for time with a tight grip, and stop the set when your grip begins to slip.
good_when: You want to build grip strength and endurance with minimal setup.
You need better grip carryover to deadlifts, rows, and pull-ups.
You want a low-fatigue finisher that is easy to progress by time or thicker bar.
bad_when: Your main goal is forearm muscle size, where loaded dynamic wrist work grows the flexors and extensors more directly.
You have shoulder or elbow issues aggravated by a dead hang.
You want to train the wrist extensors specifically, which a hang does not target.
